page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r7.3xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 12 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 12 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8369B processor running at 2.7 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The instance is configured with 96.0 GB of RAM, establishing an 8.0 GB per core ratio. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and baseline network bandwidth of 8 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations place the ecs.r7.3xlarge in the average performance tier across most metrics. It achieves a Geekbench single-core score of 1548.0 and a multi-core score of 7628.0, alongside a Passmark CPU score of 16347.91. Memory bandwidth tests show 635.68 GB/sec for cached small-block reads, dropping to 40.39 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. The CPU supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and cryptography acceleration like AES and SHA_NI, which aids in processing encrypted datasets. However, the instance exhibits weak performance in LLM inference tasks, generating only 8.09 tokens per second on a medium 7B model.

The primary tradeoff of this instance is the lack of local storage and GPU acceleration, which limits its utility for real-time graphics or large-scale machine learning training. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio provides high memory density without requiring payment for excess compute cores. This makes the instance appropriate for memory-intensive workloads such as Redis caching, where it achieves 11.89 million SET operations per second, as well as relational databases, in-memory data processing, and static web serving.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r7.3xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 12 dedicated vCPUs on an Intel Xeon 8369B processor running at 2.7 GHz, paired with 96.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the x86_64 architecture under the KVM hypervisor, this instance provides an 8.0 GB per core memory ratio but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks place the server in the average tier, with a Passmark CPU score of 16347.91 and a Redis throughput of 11.89 million SET operations per second. While its memory bandwidth is robust, reaching 635.68 GB/sec for cached reads, it shows weak performance in LLM inference tasks. The qualitative cost profile is favorable for memory-heavy workloads that do not require high compute scaling. It is best suited for in-memory databases, caching layers, and relational database hosting.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r7.3xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 12 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 96.0 GB of RAM, and 8 Gbps network bandwidth, designed for database and caching workloads.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r7.3xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 12 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 96.0 GB of RAM, and 8 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A memory-optimized x86_64 virtual server featuring a high RAM-to-vCPU ratio for database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8369B processor with 12 dedicated vCPUs and 96.0 GB of RAM.
Memory-optimized 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio suitable for in-memory data structures.
Delivers 11.89 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks.
Supports AVX-512 and SHA_NI instruction sets for accelerated cryptography.
No local storage or GPU hardware included, relying on network-attached resources.
Best suited for relational databases, Redis caching, and memory-bound applications.
